    Airbus Beluga

    Airbus · Outsize freighter · In service

    The aircraft that carries aircraft.

    Length63.1 m
    Wingspan60.3 m
    Range4,300 km
    MTOW227 t
    Built11
    First flight2018
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Range per tonne#40 of 40
    18.9 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#13 of 43
    0.96 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Airbus Beluga's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    Airbus Beluga60.3 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #16 of 43 by length → #20 by weight → #37 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length (XL)63.10 m
    Wingspan (XL)60.30 m
    Height (XL)18.90 m
    MTOW (XL)227,000 kg
    Payload (XL)51,000 kg
    Range (XL, max payload)≈4,300 km
    Engines (XL)2 × Trent 700
    First flight (XL)19 July 2018
    Built6 XL + 5 ST

    Compare it

    ← PreviousAirbus A380 Next →Antonov An-124 Ruslan

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    Airbus builds its aircraft across a continent — wings in Wales, fuselages in Germany, tails in Spain — and the Beluga is how the pieces meet in Toulouse and Hamburg. The original Beluga ST (an A300 with a whale's forehead) flew in 1994; the current BelugaXL, based on the A330, carries a full pair of A350 wings at once and wears a painted smile because Airbus staff voted for it.

    The XL's cargo bay is the largest cross-section of any aircraft flying, loaded through a huge upward-hinging forward door above the dropped cockpit. Six were built, flying a constant shuttle between Airbus plants — statistically among the hardest-working aircraft in Europe.

    The five older STs got a second career: Airbus Beluga Transport now hires them out for outsize cargo — satellites, helicopters, whatever won't fit in anything else except an An-124.

    Variants

    The Airbus Beluga line

    Beluga ST (A300-600ST)
    The 56.15 m original, 1994 — now flying charter outsize cargo.
    BelugaXL (A330-743L)
    The 63.10 m successor. Spec shown above.
    Operators

    Who flies the Airbus Beluga

    All 2 current operators, verified 2026-07-14. Fleet counts are approximate where marked ≈.

    EUAirbus Transport InternationalThe BelugaXL fleet — the internal airline shuttling parts between Airbus plants.6
    EUAirbus Beluga TransportThe ST fleet, offered commercially for outsize charter.≈4

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
